response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the role requires deep hardware-domain expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ IDE tooling experience are the closest touchpoints, but there is no evidence of deep-learning workload analysis, GPU/SIMD architecture, or silicon-level performance work expected at principal level in this domain.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, or SIMD/MIMD performance optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU expertise
